Source URL: https://www.sbdc.uh.edu/sbdc/Consulting1.asp

# SBDC Business Advising Overview

## Resource type
No-cost one-on-one professional business advising.

## Best for
Entrepreneurs who need business planning, financing, market research, management, marketing, training, strategic planning, financial analysis, international trade, government procurement, or loan proposal support.

## Best-fit BOSS lifecycle stages
Ideation, Validation, Formation, Launch, Operations, Growth, Exit/Transition, Recovery.

## Use when the entrepreneur says
"I need help planning," "I need projections," "I need funding readiness," "I need market research," "I need someone local to help me figure out what to do next."

## Resource summary
The UH SBDC business advising page describes free one-on-one advising at multiple locations, with support across the business lifecycle and expertise areas such as planning, capital access, marketing, technology commercialization, market research, strategic planning, financial analysis, international trade, government procurement, and loan proposals.

## What the user can do with this resource
Request advising, prepare for a meeting, choose a local SBDC, and work with an advisor on the business question.

## BOSS routing notes
BOSS should route here when the user needs local, structured, technical business support rather than only general education.

## Related source URLs
- https://www.sbdc.uh.edu/sbdc/Find_Your_SBDC.asp
- https://www.sbdc.uh.edu/sbdc/Locations.asp

## BOSS Library use notes
- Treat this as a resource-routing document, not as legal, tax, lending, certification, or procurement advice.
- Use the linked source URL as the user-facing official link.
- Ask discovery questions before routing when the user is early-stage or unclear.
- For Houston/77084 users, verify county/service-area fit through the official SBDC/SBA locator before giving a final local-office referral.
